content.text:
Here’s what is in place and what you still need to fix locally (tooling here could not apply the last edit). ## What was added under `apps/cli-ops` 1. **`src/services/idealista-listing-html.ts`** — deterministic Idealista PT detail-page parser: - Finds the real listing **`var config = { … }`** block (skips `var config = config || {}`), using a **brace-balanced, string-aware** slice so parsing does not depend on the whole page layout. - Reads **`propertyId`**, **`detailUrl`**, **`locale`**, **`typology`**, **`operation`**, **`adExternalReference`**, and **`idForm`** (`buyingPrice`, `locationId`) from that object. - Reads **map centre** from **`multimediaCarrousel.map.src`** (`center=lat,lng`, URL-decoded). - DOM fallbacks: **`main-info__title-main` / `title-minor`**, **`.info-features`** area, **price** spans, **`#details`** characteristic `
  • ` lines, **€/m²** row, and the **comment** `

    ` for `notes`. - Maps booleans: **`buildableYn`** if text mentions urbanizável; **`roadAccessYn`** if “Acesso por estrada”. - **`parseIdealistaPtNumber`** handles Portuguese **thousands `.`** / **decimals `,`** (e.g. `26.125` → `26125`, `80.000` → `80000`). - Builds **`id: idealista-`**, **`referenceUrl`**, fills **`rawMetrics`** with `idealista_*` keys (areas, refs, full characteristic list, parish when parsed). 2. **`src/services/scraper-service.ts`** — **`ScraperService`** (`ServiceMap`) exposing **`parseIdealistaListingHtml`**, with **`ScraperServiceLive`**. 3. **`src/commands/scrape-idealista.ts`** — subcommand **`scrape-idealista`**: - **`html-file`** path as **`Argument.string`**, reads via **`Bun.file(...).text()`** (avoids pulling extra context into `runMain`). - **`--json`** for JSON instead of YAML. - Uses **`ScraperService.use`** + **`Effect.provide(ScraperServiceLive)`**. 4. **`src/commands/subcommands.ts`** — registers **`scrapeIdealistaCommand`**. 5. **`package.json`** — **`@portugal/domain-shared`**, **`yaml`**. 6. **`tsconfig.json`** — **`"target": "ES2022"`** for modern iteration typing. ## Bug you must fix (schema decode) The candidate object sets optional fields to **`undefined`** (e.g. `koStatus`, `region`). **`decodePlotRecord`** then fails with **“Expected string, got undefined”** at `koStatus`. **Minimal fix** — change the decode line to drop `undefined` keys: ```ts return decodePlotRecord(JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(candidate))); ``` (Cleaner alternative: an **`omitUndefinedKeys`** helper and build `candidate` without those keys.) After that, a quick check from `apps/cli-ops`: ```bash bun -e "import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'; import { parseIdealistaListingHtmlToPlotRecord } from './src/services/idealista-listing-html.ts'; console.log(JSON.stringify(parseIdealistaListingHtmlToPlotRecord(readFileSync('../frontend-web/src/lib/data/plots/plot1.html','utf8')), null, 2))" ``` ## `plot1.html` vs `idealista-32605402.yaml` The saved HTML is for listing **`33531311`** (see `propertyId` / `detailUrl` in the embedded config), not **`32605402`**. The scraper **`id`** will be **`idealista-33531311`**, matching `idealista-33531311.yaml` rather than the stub `idealista-32605402.yaml`. ## CLI run / Effect resolution `bun run src/index.ts` failed in this environment with a **`effect/Context`** resolution issue via **`@effect/platform-node-shared`** (version skew in the install tree). **`tsc`** for `cli-ops` did succeed earlier; if you still see that at runtime, align **`effect`** / **`@effect/platform-*`** versions across the workspace or run from a clean **`bun install`** at the repo root. --- If you want, once the one-line decode fix is in, we can add a tiny **`bun test`** that asserts keys from `plot1.html` (price **80000**, area **26125**, coords **37.43384420, -8.34935390**, municipality **Ourique**, etc.). [REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what changed in the Idealista scraper (`apps/cli-ops/src/services/idealista-listing-html.ts`) so it lines up with **`PlotRecord`** and the **seed-style** shape in `seed-plot-001.yaml`.

### Top-level fields (same schema as seed)

| Seed field | Scraper behaviour |
|------------|-------------------|
| **name** | `og:title` / `<title>` (suffix `— idealista` stripped), else `main-info__title-main`, else `Terreno — {municipality}`. |
| **address** | When exact address is hidden: combines **map crumbs** (`header-map-list`), **headline location** (`à venda em …`), and **concelho/distrito** so you get lines like `fitos, Santana da Serra · Ourique, Beja` or `São Pedro Terena, Alandroal, Évora`. |
| **municipality** | `main-info__title-minor`, headline, or last map crumb (`X, Distrito`). |
| **region** | Derived from **district** on the last crumb via a **district → macro-region** map (e.g. Beja/Évora → **Alentejo**, Faro → **Algarve**). |
| **askingPriceEur** | Unchanged (`idForm.buyingPrice` / DOM). |
| **location** | Still from static map `center=` in `config`. |
| **koStatus** | Always **`none`** for scraped listings (matches seed usage). |
| **buildableYn** / **roadAccessYn** | Unchanged (characteristics text). |
| **utilitiesBasicYn** | New heuristic from PT keywords in characteristics + notes (água, eletricidade, esgoto, …). |
| **notes** | Unchanged (comment block). |

### `rawMetrics`: seed-like keys where the HTML supports them

Populated when grounded in the page:

- **`plot_size`** ← total plot area (m²)  
- **`permitted_buildable_area`** ← “Superfície edificável … m²”  
- **`existing_building_usability`** ← e.g. **`renovation`** when **ruína** appears in text  
- **`development_flexibility`** ← **`high`** / **`medium`** / **`low`** from urbanização / viabilidade wording  
- **`potential_two_units`** ← **`clearly possible`** vs **`unlikely`** from bifamiliar / unifamiliar cues  
- **`plot_complexity`** / **`slope`** ← light keyword heuristics  

Idealista-specific keys stay for traceability: **`idealista_*`** (including **`idealista_location_crumbs`**, **`idealista_meta_description`**, **`idealista_og_title`**, **`idealista_district`**, etc.).

### Schema decode fix

Optional fields are filled with **`omitUndefinedKeys`** before **`decodePlotRecord`**, so explicit `undefined` no longer breaks **`optionalKey`** decoding.

### Regression check

- **`plot1.html`**: region **Alentejo**, **`plot_size` 26125**, **`permitted_buildable_area` 368**, **`existing_building_usability` renovation**, etc.  
- **`idealista-34245884.html`**: address **`São Pedro Terena, Alandroal, Évora`**, **`plot_size` 80000** from meta description, headline-based parish.

What **cannot** be filled from Idealista HTML without extra APIs (so seed keys stay absent unless you add them later): **`distance_*`**, **`quietness_noise`**, **`view_quality`**, **`area_quality`**, **`orientation`**, **`access_road_quality`**, **`utilities`** (numeric), etc.—those are judgments or GIS routes, not in the listing HTML.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
I have just linked the CLI apps package binary using bunlink, and when I try to run the CLI application I get this error.

Can you please investigate why?

portugal-ops --help
error: Cannot find module 'effect/Context' from '/Users/psoudan/workspace/11-personal/portugal/node_modules/.bun/@effect+platform-node-shared@4.0.0-beta.47+622b9e60f87a09e4/node_modules/@effect/platform-node-shared/dist/NodeStream.js' @portugal/apps/cli-ops/package.json:15-17 
</user_query>

Tracing dependency versions and why `@effect/platform-node-shared` resolves to a broken `effect` import.

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: `@effect/platform-bun@4.0.0-beta.40` depends on `@effect/platform-node-shared@^4.0.0-beta.40`, which Bun resolved to `4.0.0-beta.47`. That build expects `effect@^4.0.0-beta.47` and imports `effect/Context`, while the monorepo hoists `effect@4.0.0-beta.40` — a version skew.

Pinning `@effect/platform-node-shared` to `4.0.0-beta.40` via root `overrides` (and adding an explicit dependency on `cli-ops` for clarity).

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause

1. **`@effect/platform-bun@4.0.0-beta.40`** depends on **`@effect/platform-node-shared@^4.0.0-beta.40`**.
2. Bun satisfied `^` with **`@effect/platform-node-shared@4.0.0-beta.47`**, which declares **`effect@^4.0.0-beta.47`** and imports **`effect/Context`**.
3. Your app still uses **`effect@4.0.0-beta.40`**. That mismatch led to a broken resolve under  
   `node_modules/.bun/@effect+platform-node-shared@4.0.0-beta.47/...`.

So this was **semver drift inside the Effect beta line**, not a bad `bin` path from `bun link`.

### Fix (in the repo)

1. **Root `package.json` — `overrides`**

   `"@effect/platform-node-shared": "4.0.0-beta.40"`  
   so it stays on the same line as **`effect`** and **`@effect/platform-bun`** (beta.40).

2. **`apps/cli-ops/package.json`**

   Explicit **`@effect/platform-node-shared": "4.0.0-beta.40"`** so the CLI package always declares the aligned version.

3. **`bun install`** at the repo root (lockfile updated).

4. If anything still pointed at **beta.47**, remove the stale Bun store slice under **`node_modules/.bun/`** for that package (or wipe **`node_modules`** and run **`bun install`** again). After that, **`portugal-ops --help`** runs cleanly.

### If it happens again

After changing Effect-related deps, run from the monorepo root:

```bash
rm -rf node_modules && bun install
```

(or delete only the `@effect+platform-node-shared@4.0.0-beta.47*` folder under `node_modules/.bun` if you see this exact error).

### Alternative (not applied)

You could instead **bump the whole stack** to **`4.0.0-beta.47`** (`effect`, `@effect/platform-bun`, workspace packages that depend on `effect`) so everything matches `platform-node-shared` beta.47. The override approach keeps you on **beta.40** with less churn.

[REDACTED]

Ran the linked binary against all four listings:

```bash
PLOTS="/Users/psoudan/workspace/11-personal/portugal/apps/frontend-web/src/lib/data/plots"
for id in 32605402 33531311 34219058 34245884; do
  portugal-ops scrape-idealista "$PLOTS/idealista-${id}.html" "$PLOTS/idealista-${id}.yaml"
done
```

**Result:** Exit code **0** for every pair; YAML paths match the HTML names under `apps/frontend-web/src/lib/data/plots/`.

**Checks:**  
- `idealista-32605402.yaml` — `id: idealista-32605402`, `referenceUrl` …`/32605402/`, structured fields present.  
- `idealista-33531311.yaml` — `id: idealista-33531311`, pricing/area/buildable metrics and characteristics populated.

The same command pattern was applied for **34219058** and **34245884** (outputs updated in place next to their HTML).

So you can regenerate everything anytime with:

```bash
portugal-ops scrape-idealista <path-to>.html <path-to>.yaml
```

(Default format is YAML; no extra flags needed.)

    extractNumericFactFromCharacteristicsNeedles(characteristics, [
      "superfície total do terreno",

      "minimum plot area",

content.text:
### Address / postal detail

In these four saves there is **no full street address or postcode** in the static HTML:

- The page shows **“For privacy reasons, the advertiser has not indicated the exact address.”**
- In `var config`, the map block includes **`"addressVisibility":"HIDDEN"`**, which matches that.

So you only get **human-readable location text** (parish / locality / municipality / district / breadcrumbs), which you already surface as `address`, crumbs, etc.—not a deliverable postal address.

---

### Geo coordinates

There **are** approximate coordinates: they sit in the **Google Static Maps** URL as `center=lat,lng` (comma often URL-encoded as `%2C`), for example:

`center=38.70280460%2C-7.40618460`

Because visibility is **HIDDEN**, treat that point as **Idealista’s map centroid** (often neighbourhood / municipality–level), **not** a surveyed parcel corner.

---

### Why YAML had no `location` before

The scraper tried to read `map: { src: "..." }`, but this config uses JSON-style **`"map":{"src":"..."}`**, so **`extractStaticMapCenter` never matched** and `location` was omitted.

That’s now fixed: extraction accepts the quoted `"map"` shape (and still falls back to an unquoted `map:` block or any `staticmap` URL in the config). All four `idealista-*.yaml` files were **regenerated** so they include **`location.lat` / `location.lng`** where the map URL is present.

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### Data model & scraper
- **`PlotRecord.imageUrls`** — optional `string[]` in `packages/domain-shared/src/plot-scoring/entities.ts`. First entry is the **cover** everywhere.
- **Idealista HTML** — reads `multimediaCarrousel` as JSON (balanced `{…}`), collects **`topPicture`** URLs first, then **`multimedias`** entries with `type === "PICTURE"`, using `src` / `srcWebp`. Dedupes by numeric **image id** in the URL so you don’t get the same shot twice as JPG + WebP.
- **`parsePlotRecord`** in `plot-storage.ts` loads `imageUrls` from YAML/IndexedDB.
- **Regenerated** all four `idealista-*.yaml` files (e.g. `33531311` has **3** URLs; single-photo listings have **1**).

### UI
- **`PlotPhotoCarousel`** (`apps/frontend-web/src/components/plot-photo-carousel.tsx`) — 16:10 area, prev/next, dot indicators, **ArrowLeft / ArrowRight** when focused, lazy-loaded images.
- **`plot-detail-panel.tsx`** — uses the carousel instead of `MediaHero`.
- **`plots.index.tsx`** — `coverImageSrc={plot.imageUrls?.[0]}` on `PlotSummaryCard`.
- **`plots.$plotId.tsx`** — same carousel on the full-page plot view.
- **`plot-profiles-dashboard-page.tsx`** & **`profile-detail-panel.tsx`** — `imageSrc: plot.imageUrls?.[0]` on ranking rows so dashboard thumbnails match the cover.

`bun run build` succeeds for **domain-shared**, **cli-ops**, and **frontend-web**.  

**Note:** Saved Idealista HTML only includes photos present in `multimediaCarrousel` (often **all** gallery shots for that save, but not lazy-loaded extras). If you need every listing photo, you’d have to scrape/API beyond the static HTML.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what changed in `PlotPhotoCarousel`:

### Inline carousel (plot detail & full-page plot)
- Replaced **`object-cover`** with **`object-contain`** on the main image so it stays **uncropped** and **not stretched** inside the 16:10 frame (letterboxing on `bg-muted` where aspect ratios differ).
- Wrapped the image in a **flex + center** container with **`max-h-full max-w-full`** so scaling stays uniform.
- Added **`sizes`** so the browser can pick a reasonable intrinsic layout slot (URLs are still single-resolution Idealista links).

### Fullscreen gallery (modal)
- The carousel area is a **`button`** overlay (`cursor-zoom-in`, focus ring) that opens a **`Dialog`**.
- Dialog content is **edge-to-edge** (`!fixed !inset-0`, `!h-[100dvh]`, `!w-screen`, resets translate) with a **black** surface and light-on-dark chrome.
- Lightbox image uses **`max-h-[calc(100dvh-7rem)] max-w-full object-contain`** only — **no `object-cover`** / **`background-size: cover`**, so the **whole photo** is visible; tall/wide shots can **scroll** inside the flex region (`overflow-auto`).
- Same **prev/next** and **dots** as inline mode (with `stopPropagation` so they don’t trigger the zoom button).
- **Arrow keys** move slides when the dialog is focused; **Escape** closes via Radix.
- **`DialogTitle`** + **`DialogDescription`** (`sr-only`) for accessibility.

Shared controls live in a small **`CarouselChrome`** helper with an **`inline` | `lightbox`** variant for button/dot styling on dark vs light backgrounds.
